_meta.project: DATA Project / COMP2850 Environmental Monitoring Dashboard
_meta.description: Alert threshold rules for all five sensor domains. These rules are already encoded in the synthetic datasets — your dashboard status column and alert_triggered column are derived from exactly these thresholds. Implement them as-is. You do not need to change them. If you do change a threshold, you must document your reason and cite the standard you are referencing.

status_levels.normal: All readings within acceptable range. No action required.
status_levels.warning: One or more readings approaching or exceeding a safe limit. Flag for review.
status_levels.critical: One or more readings exceed a safety or action threshold. Immediate attention required.

_meta.logic: status is the WORST level triggered by any single rule in that row. alert_triggered = 1 when status is warning or critical.

standards.SANS_241_2015: South African National Standard for drinking water quality
standards.FAO_56: FAO Irrigation and Drainage Paper 56 — crop water requirements
standards.FAO_61: FAO Irrigation and Drainage Paper 61 — agricultural drainage water quality
standards.FAO_FAW_IPM_2018: FAO Fall Armyworm Integrated Pest Management Guide 2018
standards.Huber_Gillespie_1992: Huber & Gillespie, Annual Review of Phytopathology — leaf wetness and disease
standards.Martiskainen_2009: Martiskainen et al. 2009 — livestock accelerometer behaviour classification
standards.CASAS_Cook_2013: CASAS Smart Home dataset, Cook et al. 2013 — PIR/door event calibration
standards.SANS_1691_2007: South African National Standard for CO and smoke alarm thresholds

water_quality.description: River and reservoir water quality monitoring. Sites differ in baseline contamination level — downstream is more affected by agricultural runoff than upstream.
water_quality.note_on_turbidity: Turbidity measures light scattering by suspended particles — sediment, algae, bacteria, organic matter. It is a public health proxy: turbid water cannot be reliably disinfected because particles shield bacteria from chlorine. In the Eastern Cape context, post-rainfall turbidity spikes indicate genuine pathogen transport risk, not merely aesthetic cloudiness.
water_quality.note_on_contamination_events: A simultaneous spike in BOTH turbidity AND conductivity suggests chemical or agricultural runoff contamination rather than plain sediment wash. Rain alone drives turbidity up and conductivity down (dilution effect), so a combined spike is the key signature.

warning.rationale: Outside comfortable range for drinking water

critical.rationale: Outside safe range — corrosive or scaling risk

warning.rationale: Aesthetic limit — visibly cloudy; disinfection reliability reduced

critical.rationale: Treatment required before use; significant pathogen transport risk

warning.rationale: Elevated dissolved solids — possible agricultural or industrial input

critical.rationale: High salinity — unsuitable for drinking or sensitive irrigation

water_quality.status_logic: status = critical if ANY critical rule fires; warning if ANY warning rule fires and no critical; normal otherwise.

soil_monitoring.description: Soil condition monitoring across three agricultural sites with different land use and drainage characteristics.
soil_monitoring.note_on_ec: Soil EC (electrical conductivity) rises as soil moisture falls — dissolved salts concentrate as water is removed. EC is therefore a proxy for salinity stress risk.

rules.unit: VWC — Volumetric Water Content as a fraction (0.0–1.0; multiply by 100 for %)

warning.rationale: Below field capacity lower bound (drought stress) or above upper bound (waterlogging / anaerobic risk)

critical.rationale: Severe drought stress — wilting point threshold for most crops

warning.rationale: Outside optimal range for most crops — nutrient availability affected

critical.rationale: Strongly acidic or alkaline — toxic to most crops

warning.rationale: Salinity stress beginning for sensitive crops

critical.rationale: Severe salinity — most crops significantly impaired

soil_monitoring.status_logic: status = critical if ANY critical rule fires; warning if ANY warning rule fires and no critical; normal otherwise.

pest_monitoring.description: Pest pressure and crop disease risk monitoring across three crop types. Combines trap counts (direct pest detection) with environmental conditions (disease risk modelling).
pest_monitoring.note_on_disease_model: Disease risk is a compound trigger — it requires temperature, humidity, AND sustained leaf wetness simultaneously. No single sensor reading alone triggers it. This reflects the Huber & Gillespie (1992) model widely used in phytopathology.
pest_monitoring.note_on_leaf_wetness: Leaf wetness (0–1) is dimensionless. Values above 0.6 indicate conditions where fungal spores can germinate and infect plant tissue.

warning.rationale: Action threshold — population large enough to cause measurable crop loss if untreated

warning.condition: air_temperature_c BETWEEN 15 AND 28 AND relative_humidity_pct > 75 AND leaf_wetness_0_1 > 0.6
warning.rationale: Conditions favourable for fungal disease initiation

critical.condition: above conditions sustained for >= 6 consecutive hours
critical.rationale: Sustained wetness allows spore germination and infection — high disease probability

pest_monitoring.status_logic: status = critical if pest_trap_count >= 20 OR alert_disease_high; warning if pest_trap_count >= 5 OR alert_disease_moderate (and no critical); normal otherwise.

livestock_tracking.description: GPS location and accelerometer activity data for two livestock herds. Designed to detect illness (low activity), theft (geofence breach or sudden high activity), and predator events.
livestock_tracking.note_on_accel: Accelerometer magnitude (g) reflects behaviour: ~1.0g = resting, ~1.2g = slow movement/grazing, ~1.5g = active walking, >3.5g = fleeing or struggling. Low activity alerts only fire during active hours (06:00–20:00) — animals resting at night is normal and should not alert.
livestock_tracking.note_on_geofence: The geofence is a circular boundary around the home kraal. Radius: 0.004° (~440m) for cattle, 0.006° (~660m) for goats. Breach does not always mean theft — animals may have found a gap in fencing — but it warrants investigation.

warning.condition: accel_mag_g < 1.08 sustained across 4 consecutive readings AND hour BETWEEN 6 AND 20
warning.rationale: Prolonged low activity during active hours may indicate illness, injury, or exhaustion

critical.rationale: Sudden high-magnitude movement consistent with fleeing, struggling, or theft event

livestock_tracking.status_logic: status = critical if accel > 3.5g; warning if low_activity OR geofence_breach (and no critical); normal otherwise.

infrastructure_events.description: Event log for fixed farm gate and perimeter fence infrastructure. Boolean event data — not continuous sensor readings. Designed to complement livestock_tracking.csv: correlate gate events with animal GPS proximity.

warning.rationale: Gate opened outside normal working hours — warrants attention
warning.note: No formal standard; threshold set by operational context

critical.rationale: Perimeter breach detected via vibration — always requires investigation

infrastructure_events.status_logic: status = critical if fence_breach_event = 1; warning if gate_open at night; normal otherwise.

security_events.description: Community security and emergency alert network. Multi-sensor fusion: no single sensor alone triggers a critical alert (except panic button). This reduces false positives.
security_events.note_on_fusion: The intrusion alert requires motion AND door_open AND vibration > 0.5 AND night simultaneously. This multi-sensor fusion approach is standard practice in security systems to avoid false positives from a single triggered sensor.
security_events.note_on_smoke: smoke_ppm is a proxy from the MQ135 gas sensor, not a calibrated smoke detector. The thresholds are indicative. A real deployment would require a dedicated smoke/CO sensor for life-safety applications.

critical.condition: motion_detected = 1 AND door_open = 1 AND vibration_level > 0.5 AND (hour < 6 OR hour >= 19)
critical.rationale: Multi-sensor fusion — simultaneous motion, door, and vibration at night strongly indicates intrusion

warning.rationale: Elevated smoke — possible cooking fire or early fire event

critical.rationale: Fire event likely — immediate response required
Manual distress signal — always critical, no fusion required

warning.rationale: Motion during deep night hours — not sufficient alone for intrusion alert but warrants logging

security_events.status_logic: status = critical if alert_intrusion OR alert_fire OR panic_triggered; warning if motion at night (22:00-06:00) OR smoke > 35ppm (and no critical); normal otherwise.
